claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the eligibility assessment for Child Benefit.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know which age category applies to your child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. 

My child Alex is 6 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Wonderful! Since Alex is 6 years old (under 16), that's a great start for eligibility. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week, or not in paid work at all?

*(At 6 years old, I'd expect Alex isn't working, but I need to confirm this for the assessment.)*

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all - they're only 6!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex **not** receiving any benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?

*(Again, at 6 years old this would be very unusual, but I need to confirm.)*

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you for confirming. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex currently **not** in an apprenticeship?

*(At 6 years old, this wouldn't apply, but I need to confirm for the assessment.)*

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in an apprenticeship - they're only 6!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely right! Let me move on to the next part of the assessment, which will now be about you as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now let's talk about your residency status.**

To be eligible for Child Benefit, you need to meet UK residency requirements. You can qualify if **any one** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. **You have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **You have pre-settled status** and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. **You have pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work (can claim for up to 91 days)
5. **You have pre-settled status** with sufficient financial resources (and not claiming certain benefits)
6. **You have pre-settled status** and are a student with sufficient resources
7. **You have pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. **You're a Crown servant** living abroad

Do any of these apply to you? The simplest one for most people is: **Do you normally live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Living in Manchester means you meet the UK residency requirements. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Are you responsible for Alex? You can qualify if **any one** of the following applies:

1. **You live with Alex**
2. **You contribute to Alex's upkeep** (at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount) – this includes things like food, clothes, pocket money, housing, or presents
3. **You pay maintenance** for Alex under a court order or agreement

Does any of these apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! That confirms you're responsible for Alex. Let's continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

- **No one else is claiming** for Alex
- **Someone else was claiming**, but they have agreed to transfer the claim to you
- **There is a dispute** about who should receive Child Benefit for Alex

Which situation applies to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No one else is claiming for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that keeps things straightforward. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Where does Alex currently live? I need to check if any special circumstances apply:

- **In hospital** – Alex is currently staying in hospital
- **In care** – Alex is in local authority care
- **Fostered** – Alex is being fostered
- **Living normally at home** – Alex lives at home with you in normal circumstances

Which of these best describes Alex's situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex has been in care for a couple of months, but they're not there all the time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for letting me know. Let me ask a bit more about Alex's care arrangement to determine which rules apply.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thanks for that information. Since Alex has been in care, I need to check a couple of things:

You mentioned Alex has been in care for a couple of months but isn't there all the time. To help determine eligibility, does **either** of the following apply?

1. **Alex spends at least 24 hours a week at home with you**, OR
2. **Alex spends at least 2 consecutive nights per week at home**, OR
3. **Alex has spent 7 or more days in a row at home recently**

Do any of these situations apply to Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex comes home to stay with me every weekend, so they're spending time at home with me each week.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's really helpful to know. Since Alex comes home every weekend, that means they're spending at least 2 consecutive nights per week at home with you, which allows you to continue receiving Child Benefit even though Alex is in care. Let's proceed.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Final question:**

Is the local council **NOT** paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?

In other words, are you covering Alex's costs yourself, rather than the council paying for their care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - I'm not getting anything from the council for Alex's care.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! That completes the assessment, and I have good news for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## 🎉 Great News! You ARE ELIGIBLE for Child Benefit for Alex

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

### Summary for Alex:
- ✅ Alex is under 16 years old
- ✅ Alex is not working, receiving benefits, or in an apprenticeship
- ✅ You live in the UK (Manchester)
- ✅ You are responsible for Alex (Alex lives with you)
- ✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- ✅ Although Alex is in part-time care, they come home every weekend (meeting the 2+ consecutive nights rule)
- ✅ The council is not paying for Alex's care

---

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** or adoption certificate
4. Payments are usually made **every 4 weeks** (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

### Important Note:
If you or your partner earns over **£60,000** per year, you may be affected by the **High Income Child Benefit Charge**. You can still claim, but some or all of the benefit may need to be repaid through tax.

👉 **Apply here:** [https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), which meets the age requirement. Alex is not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residency requirements. The claimant is responsible for Alex as Alex lives with them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Although Alex has been in care for a couple of months, Alex comes home every weekend (spending at least 2 consecutive nights per week at home), which maintains eligibility. The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements for Alex. Despite Alex being in part-time care, the regular weekend visits home (2+ consecutive nights per week) and the fact that the council is not paying for Alex's care means eligibility is maintained.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Has the child been in care for 8 weeks or less, OR do they spend at least 24 hours a week at home?
response.help_text: Child Benefit continues for up to 8 weeks in care. Exception if child spends 24+ hours/week at home. Can claim again if child spends 2 consecutive nights/week or 7+ days in a row at home.

age_limits.birthday_rule: Benefit stops on child's 20th birthday, not end of academic year

training_exclusions: Training with employer agreement for future job

payment_rates.note: Current rates not specified in source documents - check gov.uk for latest rates

high_income_charge.note: High Income Child Benefit Charge applies if you or partner earns over £60,000. This is a payment adjustment, not an eligibility criterion.

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements

Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), which meets the age requirement. Alex is not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residency requirements. The claimant is responsible for Alex as Alex lives with them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Although Alex has been in care for a couple of months, Alex comes home every weekend (spending at least 2 consecutive nights per week at home), which maintains eligibility. The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements for Alex. Despite Alex being in part-time care, the regular weekend visits home (2+ consecutive nights per week) and the fact that the council is not paying for Alex's care means eligibility is maintained.
